_defaults.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or mundu preferred; neat formal wear acceptable in outer areas. Women: Saree or churidar with dupatta. Modest dress required.
_defaults.photography: Permitted in outer areas. Not allowed near sanctum during rituals.

temples.name: Shri Chamundeshwari Temple
temples.description: Perched atop Chamundi Hills at 1,062 metres, this powerful Shakti shrine overlooks the city of Mysuru. The 18th-century gopuram is a landmark visible for miles. The 1,000-step climb passes the famous Nandi monolith carved in 1659 CE.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or trousers. Women: Saree or salwar. No shorts or sleeveless.
temples.photography: Allowed on the hillside. Restricted inside the sanctum.

temples.name: Virupaksha Temple, Hampi
temples.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the oldest functioning temples in India, predating the Vijayanagara Empire. The 50-metre gopuram reflected in the sacred Tungabhadra is iconic. Virupaksha (Shiva) has been worshipped here continuously since the 7th century.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or neat trousers. Women: Saree or salwar. Strict dress code inside.
temples.photography: Allowed in outer areas. Restricted inside the sanctum.

temples.name: Shri Manjunatha Swami Temple, Dharmasthala
temples.description: One of the most prominent Shaiva pilgrim centres of Karnataka. Unique for being managed by a Jain family (Heggade) while enshrining a Shaiva deity. Free meals (anna dasoha) are served to thousands of devotees daily. The monolithic Bahubali statue nearby is 39 feet tall.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or veshti mandatory. Women: Saree or salwar. Strict enforcement.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer complex. Restricted inside sanctum.

temples.name: Shri Venkataramana Temple, Mulki
temples.description: An ancient Vaishnavite temple on the banks of the Shambhavi River. The idol of Venkataramana is richly ornamented during festivals. The annual Brahmakalashotsava draws thousands of devotees from the coastal region.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred. Women: Saree or salwar.

temples.name: Shri Udupi Krishna Temple
temples.description: Consecrated by the saint-philosopher Madhvacharya in the 13th century, this is the heart of the Udupi cult of Krishna. The deity is viewed through a window of 9 holes (Kanakana Kindi), recalling the devotion of Kanakadasa. The Paryaya festival changes the priestly administration every two years.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or veshti mandatory inside. Women: Saree or salwar.
temples.photography: Allowed in outer courtyard. Restricted inside.

temples.name: Kukke Subramanya Temple
temples.description: One of the foremost Subramanya shrines in India, nestled in the Western Ghats at the confluence of Kumaradhara and Gurunadhi rivers. Devotees seek relief from sarpa dosha (snake afflictions). The Ashlesha Bali and Sarpa Samskara rituals are unique to this temple.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or veshti. Women: Saree or salwar. No shorts.

temples.name: Shri Mahalingeshwara Temple, Gokarna
temples.description: One of the most sacred Shiva shrines in India, where the Atmalinga brought by Ravana is enshrined. Gokarna is considered as sacred as Kashi, and the Mahabhaleshwar (another name for the deity here) Atmalinga is one of the most powerful Shiva lingas. A major pilgrimage on the Konkan coast.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti mandatory inside the sanctum. Women: Saree or salwar.
temples.photography: Allowed outside. Restricted inside the inner sanctum.

temples.name: Shri Kollur Mookambika Temple
temples.description: Nestled in the Western Ghats, Mookambika Devi is the presiding goddess of Kollur — one of the most powerful Shakti shrines in Karnataka. The deity combines Saraswati, Lakshmi, and Durga in one. The Adi Shankaracharya connection makes it a major scholarly pilgrimage.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or neat trousers. Women: Saree or salwar.
temples.photography: Permitted in outer areas. Restricted at the idol.

temples.name: Shri Venkateshwara Temple, Tirumakudal Narsipur
temples.description: Located at the Triveni Sangama (confluence of Cauvery, Kabini, and Spr rivers), this Vishnu temple is a powerful pilgrimage site. Ritual bathing at the Sangama is believed to wash away sins. The Nanjangud-Mysuru pilgrim route passes through here.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred. Women: Saree or salwar.

temples.name: Shri Nanjundeshwara Temple, Nanjangud
temples.description: Known as Dakshina Kashi (Kashi of the South), Nanjundeshwara (the Lord who swallowed poison) is enshrined here on the banks of the Kabini. The 24-metre Dravidian gopuram built by Hyder Ali is magnificent. One of the most revered Shiva temples of Karnataka.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred. Women: Saree or salwar. No shorts.

temples.name: ISKCON Temple, Bengaluru
temples.description: One of the largest ISKCON temples in the world, it serves as a major spiritual and cultural centre for Bengaluru. The grand temple complex features a Vedic museum, guest house, and cultural programmes. Janmashtami celebrations here draw over a lakh devotees.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or neat clothes. Women: Saree or salwar. Shorts and sleeveless not allowed.
temples.photography: Allowed in outer complex. Restricted inside the main shrine.

temples.name: Shri Dodda Ganapathi Temple, Bengaluru
temples.description: Home to a massive 18-foot monolithic Ganesha idol — one of the largest in India — sitting on a 15-foot pedestal. The idol was carved from a single granite boulder during the reign of Kempe Gowda in the 16th century. A cherished city landmark.
temples.dressCode: Men: Neat clothes. Women: Saree or salwar.

temples.name: Shri Nrupatunga Temple (Biligiri Rangaswamy)
temples.description: Atop the Biligiri Ranga Hills (BR Hills) in a tiger reserve, this Vishnu temple is surrounded by dense forests and tribal communities. The Soliga tribe has long traditions associated with the deity. The sunrise from the hilltop is extraordinary.
temples.dressCode: Traditional attire preferred.

temples.name: Shri Ranganathaswamy Temple, Srirangapatna
temples.description: On an island in the Cauvery River, this historic Vishnu temple was the focal point of Tipu Sultan's capital. The temple was renovated by the Hoysalas and is one of the 108 Divya Desams. Srirangapatna is a site of immense historical and spiritual significance.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred. Women: Saree or salwar.

temples.name: Shri Lakshmi Devi Temple, Doddagaddavalli
temples.description: A stunning 12th-century Hoysala temple dedicated to Goddess Lakshmi, featuring the signature star-shaped platform and intricate sculptural friezes of the Hoysala style. A UNESCO World Heritage Site (2023). One of the finest examples of medieval Indian temple architecture.
temples.dressCode: Smart casual. Respectful attire inside.

temples.name: Chennakesava Temple, Belur
temples.description: Built by the Hoysala king Vishnuvardhana in 1117 CE to commemorate his victory over the Cholas. The temple took over 100 years to complete. The sculpted exterior is unmatched — every inch carved with divine figures, friezes of elephants, lions, and scrollwork. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred inside. Women: Saree or salwar.

temples.name: Hoysaleshwara Temple, Halebidu
temples.description: The largest Hoysala temple in existence and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Twin shrines — Hoysaleshwara and Shanthala — are covered in over 240 metres of exquisitely carved friezes. No two panels are alike. One of India's greatest sculptural achievements.
temples.dressCode: Respectful attire. Men: Trousers or dhoti. Women: Salwar or saree.

temples.name: Shri Sringeri Sharadamba Temple
temples.description: Seat of the first of the four Shankaracharya Mathas established by Adi Shankaracharya in the 8th century. The Vidyashankara temple and the Sharadamba temple are the twin jewels of Sringeri — a centre of Advaita scholarship for over 1,200 years.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or neat clothes. Women: Saree or salwar. Strict standards.

temples.name: Shri Durgaparameshwari Temple, Kateel
temples.description: On an island in the Nandini River, this powerful Shakti temple is one of the most celebrated in coastal Karnataka. The presiding deity Durgaparameshwari is worshipped by both Hindus and some sections of the Jain community. The annual Brahmakalashotsava is a major regional festival.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti or veshti. Women: Saree or salwar. Shorts strictly prohibited.

temples.name: Shri Mahaganapathi Temple, Idagunji
temples.description: One of the three major Ganesha temples of the Konkan coast (along with Kaivara and Kaginele). The presiding Ganapathi idol here is svayambhu (self-manifested). The temple draws millions of devotees during Ganesh Chaturthi and is the starting point of the Parashurama Kshetra pilgrimage.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred. Women: Saree or salwar.

temples.name: Shri Brahmi Durgaparameshwari Temple, Aane Gudde
temples.description: A celebrated Shakti shrine on a hillock in the Tulu Nadu region. The deity Durgaparameshwari draws massive crowds during the Navarathri season. The annual Jatra festival is one of the most colourful in coastal Karnataka.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred. Women: Saree or salwar.

temples.name: Shri Veerabhadra Temple, Lepakshi
temples.description: A spectacular 16th-century Vijayanagara temple famous for its mural paintings, hanging pillar (which does not rest on the ground), and a massive monolithic Nandi. The Nagalinga with seven hoods and the natya mandapa with 70 pillars are architectural marvels.
temples.dressCode: Respectful attire.

temples.name: Shri Panchalingeshwara Temple, Govindanahalli
temples.description: A cluster of five Shiva temples on a rocky hillside near Tumakuru, associated with the five Pandava brothers. The hilltop views of the Deccan plateau are exceptional. An important pilgrimage for the Veerashaiva community.
temples.dressCode: Traditional attire preferred.

temples.name: Shri Siddalingeshwara Temple, Sidhaganga
temples.description: A renowned Veerashaiva (Lingayat) pilgrimage centre on a rocky hill. The Sidhaganga Math here runs one of the largest free-feeding programmes in the country, serving thousands daily. The cave shrine atop the hill is a place of deep spiritual retreat.
temples.dressCode: Traditional attire preferred.

temples.name: Shri Brahmadeva Temple, Kambadahalli
temples.description: A rare temple dedicated to Lord Brahma (the Creator), belonging to the Ganga period and featuring beautiful sculpture. One of the very few functioning Brahma temples in India. Located amidst serene Deccan countryside.
temples.dressCode: Respectful attire.

temples.name: Shri Mallikarjuna Swamy Temple, Aihole
temples.description: Aihole is the 'cradle of Indian temple architecture' — over 125 temples of different styles from the 4th to 12th centuries stand here. The Mallikarjuna group and the Durga Temple (cave temple with apsidal plan) are the most significant. A UNESCO Tentative List site.
temples.dressCode: Respectful attire.

temples.name: Shri Banashankari Temple, Badami
temples.description: One of the oldest Shakti shrines in North Karnataka, predating the Chalukya period. The presiding deity Banashankari (forest-dwelling Parvati) is the tutelary goddess of the region. The Shivaratri festival draws lakhs of devotees. Famous for the deepotsava (festival of lamps) on the sacred tank.
temples.dressCode: Men: Dhoti preferred. Women: Saree or salwar.

temples.name: Shri Gavi Gangadhareshwara Temple, Bengaluru
temples.description: A 9th-century Shiva temple built inside a natural cave, commissioned by Kempe Gowda I. During Makara Sankranti (January 14), sunlight falls directly on the Shivalinga through two disc-shaped windows — a precise astronomical alignment. One of Bengaluru's most ancient and revered temples.
temples.dressCode: Men: Neat clothes. Women: Saree or salwar.
